name: Minneapolis (United States)

colors.symbolism: the central pennant and the four civic emblems on the central white disc

description: A swallow-tailed white field charged with a centred royal-blue pennant on which is set a white disc divided into four quadrants — each containing a blue emblem: a building (education and the arts), a cogwheel and square (labour and industry), a pilot wheel (the lakes and rivers), and a microscope (research and skilled craftsmanship).
history: Adopted by the Minneapolis City Council on 27 May 1955.

features.symbolism: a blue pile spanning the flag from the hoist (full height) to a point at the centre of the fly, on a white swallow-tailed field; bears the four civic-virtue emblems

features.role: central white disc divided into four quadrants
features.symbolism: white disc set on the blue pennant, divided into four equal quadrants by a thin cross — each quadrant bearing one of the four civic emblems
represents Minneapolis's lakes and rivers and all activities identified with them
represents research, skilled craftsmanship and progress
